description_long: This is a large, sleek, gray military-style airplane with a streamlined fuselage and sharply swept wings. The wings are mounted low on the body and feature a pronounced anhedral angle, giving the aircraft a distinctive aggressive stance. The tail section includes twin vertical stabilizers and a horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail configuration. Four jet engines are mounted in pairs under the wings, close to the fuselage, indicating high thrust capability. The cockpit is positioned at the very front of the fuselage and is fully enclosed, with a smooth, aerodynamic canopy. The surface of the airplane is matte and hard, with minimal visible markings or color variation.
description: A large, gray, swept-wing military airplane with twin vertical stabilizers, four underwing jet engines, and a forward enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, twin vertical stabilizers, and the streamlined fuselage.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the elongated fuselage, low-mounted swept wings, and the placement of the engines under the wings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal stabilizer, and the engine exhausts.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the low-mounted wings, engine placement, and the aerodynamic nose.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with sharply swept wings and pronounced anhedral angle; no winglets visible.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing jet engines, nacelle-mounted in pairs close to the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a single horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear unit.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, fully enclosed cockpit with a streamlined canopy, likely multi-seat.
